__fixture__.name: destructive_remove_no_checkpoint_review
__fixture__.scenario: Pre-apply review packet for a remove_existing_dependency action that has no recoverable checkpoint. The packet resolves to rollback_unavailable_destructive_remove_with_no_checkpoint and review_blocked_pending_user_decision so the user explicitly admits or dismisses the destructive remove. Validates that destructive removes never auto-apply and that the surface discloses rollback unavailability before apply.
contract_sections: The package-review packet (rollback_unavailable_destructive_remove_with_no_checkpoint admissible only on remove_existing_dependency)
acceptance_bullets: 1: destructive remove discloses rollback unavailability before apply

decision_note: Remove blocked: no recoverable checkpoint exists for the targeted dependency. Rollback would be unavailable; the user must explicitly admit or dismiss.
registry_disclosure: Private internal registry; secret-broker handle authenticates the read.
script_risk_disclosure: Remove does not run install scripts.

lockfile_impact_envelope.lockfile_impact_disclosure: Three lockfile entries (one direct dependency plus two transitives) would be removed.

transitive_impact_envelope.transitive_impact_disclosure: Two transitive entries would be removed.

rollback_posture_disclosure: No recoverable checkpoint exists; rollback would be unavailable. The user must admit or dismiss explicitly.
